description_long: This is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, primarily constructed from polished wood with metal and bakelite components. The front features two prominent metallic bells above a rotary dial and a mouthpiece, with a wooden shelf below for writing or resting items. A black handset, connected by a straight cord, hangs on a metal hook to the left side, while a hand-crank is mounted on the right. The back is flat and unadorned, designed for mounting against a wall. The overall design is ornate, with decorative woodwork at the top and a robust, boxy structure typical of early 20th-century telephones.
description: A vintage wall-mounted wooden corded telephone with metal bells, rotary dial, and a side-mounted crank. Features a black handset on a hook and a small wooden shelf.
description_view_0: Side view showing the handset hanging on a metal hook and the wooden body profile.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the metal bells, rotary dial, mouthpiece, and the small wooden shelf.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the hand-crank and the cord connection to the handset.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat, unadorned back panel suitable for wall mounting.
